title: Assessing Global Partners (GLP) Valuation After Recent Price Moves And Long Term Return Track Record
content:
Track your investments for FREE with Simply Wall St, the portfolio command center trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

Global Partners (GLP) is drawing fresh attention after recent trading left the units at $45. For income focused investors, the long term total return profile and current value metrics are now back in focus.

See our latest analysis for Global Partners.

The recent 1 day share price return of 2.55% and 7 day share price return of 1.42% come after a 30 day share price return of a 7.22% decline, while longer term total shareholder returns of 83.08% over three years and 209.35% over five years indicate momentum built over time.

If this kind of move has you thinking about what else is out there, it could be a good moment to scan for other energy related ideas via the 28 power grid technology and infrastructure stocks

With units at $45, an intrinsic value estimate that implies a discount of about 38% and a small gap to the $45.50 analyst target, you have to ask: Is Global Partners undervalued, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 1% Undervalued

With Global Partners units at $45 and the most followed fair value estimate anchored at $45.50, the narrative frames the current pricing as very close to its calculated worth, with a small discount that hinges on a specific earnings and revenue path.

In order for you to agree with the analysts, you'd need to believe that by 2029, revenues will be $43.0 billion, earnings will come to $168.0 million, and it would be trading on a PE ratio of 11.4x, assuming you use a discount rate of 8.0%.

Read the complete narrative.Read the complete narrative.

Curious what underpins that $45.50 fair value when today’s earnings and margins look very different from those implied future numbers? The narrative highlights rapid top line expansion, steady profitability and a lower future earnings multiple as the factors used to make the valuation math work. The focus is on how these three elements are expected to interact over time, rather than any single headline figure.

Result: Fair Value of $45.50 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, you also need to weigh risks, including long term fossil fuel demand pressure and potential asset strain if terminal and station utilization weakens.

Find out about the key risks to this Global Partners narrative.

Next Steps

The mix of long term returns, valuation views and future assumptions here may feel balanced but still incomplete. Treat this as your starting point, move quickly to weigh both sides, and review the 2 key rewards and 3 important warning signs in the 2 key rewards and 3 important warning signs

Story Continues

Looking for more investment ideas?

title: Most and least shorted energy stocks with up to $2B market cap as of March
content:
In March, the S&P 500 energy sector (XLE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XLE]) moved 7.4% in the upward direction this year, supported by a whopping 44.5% rise in crude oil futures (CL1:COM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CL1:COM#hasComeFromMpArticle=false#source=section%3Amain_content%7Cbutton%3Abody_link%7Cfirst_level_url%3Anews]) over potential disruption in global crude supply.

Against this backdrop, short interest trends among micro to small-cap energy stocks show a wide divergence. Sable Offshore (SOC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SOC]) was the most shorted stock in March, while KNOT Offshore Partners (KNOP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KNOP]) was the least shorted stock.

MOST SHORTED ENERGY STOCKS AS OF MARCH END:

 	* Green Plains (GPRE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GPRE]) 20.07%
 	* New Fortress Energy (NFE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NFE]) 17.62%
 	* W&T Offshore (WTI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WTI]) 15.28%
 	* Vitesse Energy (VTS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VTS]) 13.76%
 	* Gevo (GEVO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GEVO]) 13.33%

LEAST SHORTED ENERGY STOCKS AS OF MARCH END:

 	* KNOT Offshore Partners (KNOP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KNOP]) 0.54%
 	* Tsakos Energy Navigation (TEN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TEN]) 0.99%
 	* Global Partners (GLP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GLP]) 1.06%
 	* Uni-Fuels (UFG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/UFG]) 1.25%
 	* Flowco (FLOC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FLOC]) 1.35%

Energy ETFs: (XLE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XLE]), (AMLP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMLP]), (VDE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VDE]), (XOP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XOP]), (OIH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OIH]), and (IXC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IXC]).

title: Top 10 small-cap energy stocks ranked by dividend yield
content:
[Oil pump in western Kazakhstan]
NiseriN/iStock via Getty Images

As the market grapples with volatility, below is a list of the top 10 small-cap energy stocks based in the United States, ranked by their forward dividend yield.

The list is topped by CrossAmerica Partners LP (CAPL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CAPL]), with a dividend yield of 10.08%. Kimbell Royalty Partners, LP (KRP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KRP]) and Vitesse Energy, Inc. (VTS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VTS]) are next, with TXO Partners, L.P. (TXO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TXO]) and Granite Ridge Resources, Inc. (GRNT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GRNT]) rounding out the rest of the top five. Diversified Energy Company (DEC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DEC]), Global Partners LP (GLP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GLP]) and Riley Exploration Permian, Inc. (REPX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/REPX]) are among the other high-yielding stocks on the list.

The list concludes with VAALCO Energy, Inc. (EGY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EGY]) and World Kinect Corporation (WKC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WKC]), which offer dividend yields of 3.99% and 3.41%, respectively.

Here is the list:

 	*
CrossAmerica Partners LP Common Units (CAPL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CAPL]), Dividend yield: 10.08%

 	*
Kimbell Royalty Partners, LP Common Units (KRP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KRP]), Dividend yield: 10.03%

 	*
Vitesse Energy, Inc. (VTS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VTS]), Dividend yield: 9.67%

 	*
TXO Partners, L.P. Common Units (TXO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TXO]), Dividend yield: 9.58%

 	*
Granite Ridge Resources, Inc. (GRNT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GRNT]), Dividend yield: 7.68%

 	*
Diversified Energy Company (DEC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DEC]), Dividend yield: 6.79%

 	*
Global Partners LP Common Units (GLP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GLP]), Dividend yield: 6.76%

 	*
Riley Exploration Permian, Inc. (REPX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/REPX]), Dividend yield: 4.37%

 	*
VAALCO Energy, Inc. (EGY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EGY]), Dividend yield: 3.99%

 	*
World Kinect Corporation (WKC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WKC]), Dividend yield: 3.41%
